name: or_scheduling
description: an operating-room scheduling world: cases with durations and rooms; actions book, delay, cancel without double-booking

card.description: an operating-room scheduling world: cases with durations and rooms; actions book, delay, cancel without double-booking

rules: A case occupies the half-open interval [start, start + duration) in exactly one room.

book schedules a pending case; it is allowed only if the interval lies within the room's open hours [0, horizon) and does not overlap any case already booked in that room. Such a request never double-books: an infeasible booking is refused and increments 'rejected' instead, leaving the schedule unchanged.
When book omits room/start, the earliest non-overlapping slot (scanning rooms in order, earliest start first) is chosen deterministically.
delay moves an already-booked case to a strictly later start in the same room, only when the shifted interval still fits without overlap; otherwise it is refused.
cancel returns a booked case to 'pending' and frees its room and start time.
booked, pending, utilization (booked room-hours) and rejected are derived counters recomputed every step. Invariant: no two cases ever overlap in the same room.

emit.report: OR schedule: {booked} cases booked, {pending} pending, {utilization} room-hours used, {rejected} requests refused (no double-booking).
